title: Unlinked task error from broken context in V3Width
body:
[This](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/12014691/t_class_dep.zip) test fails with:
```
%Error: Internal Error: t/t_class_dep.v:31:9: ../V3Task.cpp:206: Unlinked task
                                            : ... In instance $unit.B
-node: TASKREF 0xfa0b00 <e995> {e31aj} @dt=0xf87830@(w0)void  srandom -> UNLINKED
   31 |    this.srandom(0);
      |         ^~~~~~~
```

This is because V3Width doesn't always visit nodes in hierarchical order. For instance, FTaskRef visitor [visits also the referred task](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/23fe5c1b9386228afe80a42b95e91cde8462d1c4/src/V3Width.cpp#L5639), which can be defined in a different class or module. When such task is visited, the context variables like m_pkgp or m_classp point to wrong objects.

As a result, if the task contains a call to `this.srandom()` the definition of `srandom()` is inserted into a wrong class, which later causes linking problems.

Similar problem occurs [here](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/12014797/t_std_process_self.zip) with two different possible outcomes:
1. Routines from std are not marked as isFromStd [here](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/23fe5c1b9386228afe80a42b95e91cde8462d1c4/src/V3Width.cpp#L5318), because of wrong m_pkgp value, and so things like usage of process::self is not detected,
2. Verilator crashes [here](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/23fe5c1b9386228afe80a42b95e91cde8462d1c4/src/V3Width.cpp#L2639) as if the node was already widthed out of order.

Tested on the newest commit (https://github.com/verilator/verilator/commit/23fe5c1b9386228afe80a42b95e91cde8462d1c4) on Fedora with kernel 6.3.11.
